IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: TO ASSIST IN THE OPERATION OF DECENT, SAFE, AFFORDABLE HOUSING FOR LOW INCOME PERSONS.
IRS990.MissionDesc: TO ASSIST IN THE OPERATION OF DECENT, SAFE, AFFORDABLE HOUSING FOR LOW INCOME PERSONS.
IRS990.Desc: MULTIFAMILY HOUSING DEVELOPMENT - DEVELOPMENT AND OPERATION OF HOUSING FOR RENT TO VARIOUS LOW TO MODERATE INCOME PERSONS. DURING 2016, ONE NEW PROPERTY WAS ACQUIRED AND ONE NEW PROPERTY WAS CONSTRUCTED TO BE OPERATED AS A MULTIFAMILY APARTMENT COMPLEX FOR RENTAL TO LOW TO MODERATE INCOME INDIVIDUALS AND FAMILIES.

ProgSrvcAccomActy2Grp.Desc: COMMUNITY RELATIONS - TO ASSIST THE COMMUNITY THROUGH PUBLIC EDUCATION PROGRAMS.

ProgSrvcAccomActy3Grp.Desc: PROFESSIONAL SERVICES - TO PROVIDE PROFESSIONAL SERVICES TO APPROXIMATELY 36 AFFILIATED ORGANIZATIONS INCLUDING ASSET MANAGEMENT, RESIDENT SERVICES,AND OTHER ADMINISTRATIVE SERVICES.

ProgSrvcAccomActyOtherGrp.Desc: SINGLE FAMILY HOME OWNERSHIP PROGRAMS - FUNDING FOR THE ACQUSITION, SUBSTANTIAL RENOVATION AND MAINTENANCE OF HOUSES IN TARGET AREAS & ELIGIBLE LOW INCOME PERSONS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE ORGANIZATION HOLDS FUNDS DEPOSITED IN A SAVINGS ACCOUNT WITH A BALANCE WHICH IS AT LEAST EQUAL TO THE TENANTS' SECURITY DEPOSITS. TENANT SECURITY DEPOSITS ARE HELD IN A FEDERALLY INSURED BANK.

AHC has applied for and received a determination letter from the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) to be treated as a tax-exempt entity pursuant to Section 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code and did not have any unrelated business income for the years ended December 31, 2016 and 2015. Due to its tax- exempt status, AHC is not subject to income taxes. AHC is required to file tax returns with the IRS and other taxing authorities. Accordingly, these financial statements do not reflect a provision for income taxes and AHC has no other tax positions which must be considered for disclosure.
ELIMINATION OF INTERCOMPANY TRANSACTIONS 2,060,729. ELIMINATION OF AFFILIATES' REVENUE -2,677,362.

Effective January 1, 2015, AHC established a senior management deferred compensation plan that qualifies as a "top-hat plan" under IRC 457(f). Under the plan, AHC will provide a deferred compensation benefit to long-term senior management employees who are designated for participation, and who have and are expected to materially contribute to the continued growth, development and success of the Organization. The deferred compensation expense is accrued over the period beginning at the entry date for each participant through the date that each participant is 100% vested. The primary vesting date for participants is the later of the date they reach 65 years of age, the date they have been employed by AHC, Inc. for 15 years, and January 1, 2020. The current participants that will vest benefits under the deferred compensation plan when their primary vesting date has been reached are Catherine Bucknam, Carlton Miller, James Ponder, Stephen Smith, Walter Webdale and John Welsh.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section A, line 1
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS IS COMPOSED OF UP TO EIGHTEEN MEMBERS. THE OFFICERS INCLUDE PRESIDENT OF THE CORPORATION, CHAIR, VICE CHAIR, ONE OR MORE VICE-PRESIDENTS, TREASURER, SECERETARY AND OTHER OFFICERS IF NECESSARY. THE EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE, COMPOSED OF CHAIR, VICE CHAIR, PRESIDENT AND TWO OTHER MEMBERS OF THE BOARD, HAS AUTHORITY BETWEEN BOARD MEETINGS TO MAKE DECISIONS ON ITS OWN. SUBSEQUENTLY, EACH BOARD MEMBER SHALL BE PROMPTLY NOTIFIED OF SUCH ACTION. OTHER COMMITTEES, ESTABLISHED BY THE BOARD, ARE COMPOSED OF MEMBERS OF THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS. IN ADDITION TO THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS, THE ORGANIZATION IS COMPOSED OF MEMBERS. THERE ARE THREE LEVELS OF MEMBERSHIP BASED ON THE AMOUNT OF ANNUAL DUES PAID. EACH MEMBER HAS THE SAME RIGHTS AND PRIVILEGES AND IS ENTITLED TO ONE VOTE. THE LESSER OF 25 MEMBERS OR 10% OF THE MEMBERSHIP, MAY, BY WRITTEN NOTICE, REQUIRE THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS TO ORDER A SPECIAL MEETING OF THE MEMBERS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section A, line 6
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: AHC HAS BETWEEN 300-400 MEMBERS. THE MEMBERS PARTICIPATE IN THE GOVERNANCE OF THE ORGANIZATION IN THEIR CAPACITY TO ELECT THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section A, line 7a
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: AHC HAS BETWEEN 300-400 MEMBERS. THE MEMBERS PARTICIPATE IN THE GOVERNANCE OF THE ORGANIZATION IN THEIR CAPACITY TO ELECT THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section B, line 11b
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE DRAFT TAX RETURN IS REVIEWED BY THE CORPORATE OFFICERS WHO RESOLVE ANY QUESTIONS BEFORE FINALIZING THE RETURN. A COPY OF THE FINALIZED 990 IS PROVIDED TO THE FULL BOARD OF DIRECTORS BEFORE FILING WITH THE IRS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section B, line 12c
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: AHC, INC. MONITORS AND ENFORCES COMPLIANCE THROUGH CONTRACTS MANAGEMENT OR SELF DISCLOSURE, CONFLICTS ARE IDENTIFIED AND RELATED PARTIES ARE EXCLUDED FROM THE DECISION MAKING PROCESS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section B, line 15
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: A COMPENSATION STUDY ANALYSIS IS PERFORMED FOR TOP POSITIONS. A COMPENSATION CONSULTANT WAS USED TO DETERMINE MARKET RATE COMPENSATION AS RELATED TO JOB DESCRIPTIONS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section C, line 19
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THESE DOCUMENTS ARE AVAILABLE UPON REQUEST.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART IX, LINE 16 OCCUPANCY EXPENSE:
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: MODEL UNIT $ 16,800 UTILITIES 1,073,700 REPAIRS AND MAINTENANCE 510,028 REAL ESTATE TAXES 836,874 PROPERTY AND LIABILITY INSURANCE 244,972 CONTRACTS 817,460 MORTGAGE INTEREST EXPENSE 3,782,090 CONDOMINIUM FEES 116,289 ___________ TOTAL OCCUPANCY EXPENSE $ 7,398,213
THE PROCESS HAS NOT CHANGED FROM THE PRIOR YEAR.
The tax return is being amended to apply to Line H(c) the Group Exemption Number which was inadvertenly omitted.

DirectControllingEntityName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: Woods at Victoria Park Housing Corporation

RelatedOrganizationName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: Woods At Victoria Park Housing Corp
